title: What Would I Charge If Nobody Would Flinch?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for uncovering the fear underneath your pricing
excerpt: A journal set for the pricing fear that keeps rates below where they want to be. Five prompts moving from what you currently charge to the number you would name if response were guaranteed.
sourceNotes: Original journal prompt set written for homemade.education.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the gap between your current prices and your wanted prices has been running for a while without closing.

content.text: A journal set for the number you haven't said yet. The prompts move from what you currently charge, to the fear underneath the gap, to the rate you would name if no one could say no.
What do you currently charge, and what do you want to charge?
Write the numbers. Both of them. Then write for three minutes about what the gap looks like from the inside.
What happens when you imagine saying the higher number?
Locate it physically if you can. Write what arrives — tightening, avoidance, the urge to explain or discount before they respond.
What are you afraid would happen if you charged more?
Name the specific fear: that they would say no, that they would think you are too much, that you would lose the relationship, that you don't deserve it.
Who do you imagine flinching — and why do they get to decide?
Write about the imaginary audience watching your pricing. Name them. Examine how much power you have handed them.
What would you charge if nobody would flinch — including you?
Write the number. Then write one sentence about what it would feel like to say it calmly, without apology, and mean it.
